Confirm against a playtest of the live 15/28 build first (the "~1/game" the user saw may be the pre-S76 35/60 build).
2. **Global-reach entities fog-EXEMPT (visible to ALL players)** — the potato bomb + Voltkin (and **EVERY entity/effect that affects, or CAN affect, all players**) must render through the fog to everyone, not just the owner or players within vision radius. Audit all entities/effects for global/cross-player reach (potato AoE, Voltkin creature, rainbow shuffle, pickup-bomb, hunter) and make the reaching ones always-visible. Fog-rendering change — relates to backlog #3 (EYES fog); likely a Standard/Full PDR. **Rule of thumb: visible-to-all iff can-affect-all.**

## Recent Reflexion (last 2 sessions)
**S76** — hunter 2.5× faster (P1 `cbdf67c`, Micro) + rainbow more common (P2 `7d7a2bd`, Micro) + complexity-INCOME scoring (P3 `16023ae`, FULL): 3 playtest fixes, autonomous (user approved batch + left). P3 replaced the monotonic per-placement accumulator with a per-tick income whose rate ∝ standing structure complexity (#prims + 2×#magicBonds) — so destroying a structure now slows your gain, and `scoreProgress = max(scoreByPlayer)` is unified for all modes (fixes the player-1 inconsistency by construction). The PLAN Council caught a perverse-incentive bug in the first complexity formula (functional-bonding penalty) before any code; the corrected formula reproduces the old accumulator for tree builds so the 50-pt gate + 75% hunter trigger needed zero recalibration. CHECK Triumvirate: Grok SHIP + Gemini FIX-REFUTED with evidence (a cross-player-bond non-determinism that can't occur — cross-colour bonds don't form). Gates: tsc 0 / vitest 1142 / build 534.35 KB / Playwright gating 32 pass + 1 pre-existing skip. NO protocol bump.

**S75** — hot-potato re-pickup + carrier-bench (P1 `b8c5ae3`, Standard) + hunter 5×-slower (P2 `b63f5d2`, Micro) + rainbow colour-shuffle (P3 `1085632`, FULL, protocol 5→6): 3 playtest fixes + a pre-approved Rule-16 amendment.
